tenantScopeNote: Commands currently run against the default ERP Experts tenant unless a command explicitly supports --tenant.

commands.description: Daily local operator startup. Checks health, monitor state and current Sentinel summary.
commands.recommendedUsage: Run at the start of an operator session.

commands.description: Fast diagnostic for Git state, DB integrity, reports, API availability and route guard expectations.
commands.recommendedUsage: Run when something feels off or before troubleshooting.

commands.description: Checks tenant config, SQLite readiness, reports, QA totals and runtime ignore policy.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use before deployment planning or platform maintenance.

commands.description: Generates the persisted operational summary that explains what Sentinel currently knows.
commands.recommendedUsage: Refresh the operator state panel and current recommendation.

commands.description: Creates Matthew's private daily operator report from persisted Sentinel state.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use for the daily operator handoff.

commands.description: Creates a stakeholder-safe weekly SEO and content progress report.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use when preparing a business-facing update.

commands.description: Generates the local Sentinel improvement roadmap from feedback, activity and operational state.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use after feedback triage to decide the next Sentinel platform improvement.

commands.description: Turns a selected Sentinel roadmap item into a reviewable implementation plan without coding.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use before implementing a Sentinel platform improvement.

commands.description: Records local approval metadata for a selected roadmap plan before implementation work.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use after reviewing a roadmap plan and before starting implementation.

commands.description: Generates a Codex-ready implementation brief only when the selected roadmap item has active approval.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use after approval to prepare a reviewable implementation brief without applying changes.

commands.description: Exports one Codex-ready work package from an active approved implementation brief.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use as the final operator-controlled handoff before a separate implementation task.

commands.description: Reviews an exported work package for approval, scope and safety before implementation handoff.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use immediately before handing a work package to Codex for implementation.

commands.description: Tracks implementation lifecycle state for an approved and reviewed Sentinel work package.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use to mark handoff lifecycle states such as ready, in_progress, implemented or validated.

commands.description: Runs the safe local cadence: monitor, state, daily report, stakeholder report and notification payloads.
commands.recommendedUsage: Run for the scheduled local refresh workflow.

commands.description: Runs the SEO intelligence chain and recommends the next safe strategic action.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use when refreshing SEO intelligence and execution planning context.

commands.description: Read-only gate that checks whether Sentinel is safe to promote towards Raspberry Pi deployment.
commands.recommendedUsage: Run before any deployment or service work.

commands.description: Verifies the local SQLite DB and expected backup path without mutating data.
commands.recommendedUsage: Run before trusting backup readiness.

commands.description: Copies the DB to a temporary restore-test file and validates it without overwriting anything.
commands.recommendedUsage: Run to prove recovery confidence before deployment work.

commands.description: Previews the read-only Sentinel state API contract from SQLite.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use when inspecting the future API payload without starting the HTTP server.

commands.description: Starts the local read-only Sentinel HTTP API on localhost for private experiments.
commands.recommendedUsage: Use only locally. Do not expose publicly until auth exists.
